description: Fix `DatabaseFingerprintProvider` so permanent `dms.EffectiveSchema` validation failures are handled as per-database fail-fast conditions instead of being retried on every request. Acceptance: malformed singleton states such as duplicate rows, wrong `EffectiveSchemaSingletonId`, invalid `EffectiveSchemaHash`, or invalid `ResourceKeySeedHash` length are classified distinctly from transient connection/command failures; permanent fingerprint validation failures are cached per connection string or otherwise prevented from re-querying the database on every request; genuine transient read failures still retry on the next request; concurrent first-use requests still collapse to a single database read.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering permanent malformed fingerprint states and transient reader failures in `DatabaseFingerprintProviderTests` and/or `DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests`.
Verify that repeated requests against the same malformed fingerprint state do not invoke the reader repeatedly, while a transient exception still allows a later successful retry.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DatabaseFingerprintProviderTests"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests"`

description: Return actionable provisioning/fingerprint errors for malformed `dms.EffectiveSchema` content instead of falling through to a generic 500. Acceptance: missing table and missing singleton row continue to produce the existing provisioning response; malformed singleton content (for example duplicate rows or invalid fingerprint column values) produces a deterministic `application/problem+json` response that identifies the database fingerprint/provisioning problem and gives the operator an actionable remediation path; logs include the selected DMS instance and trace/correlation information; generic 500 handling is reserved for unexpected failures only.
steps-to-verify: Add middleware or pipeline tests covering malformed fingerprint cases such as duplicate rows and invalid hash/seed data, and assert the exact response type/status/body shape.
Verify that the malformed-fingerprint path no longer falls through `CoreExceptionLoggingMiddleware` as an unknown error.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware|FullyQualifiedName~ProblemDetailsResponseTests"`

description: Remove duplicated and redundant fingerprint-validation code so the runtime path has a single source of truth and fewer defensive checks. Acceptance: `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` no longer repeats instance/connection-string checks already guaranteed by `ResolveDmsInstanceMiddleware` and documented pipeline ordering; `EffectiveSchema` table metadata is defined in one shared place rather than duplicated between `DmsTableNames` and `EffectiveSchemaTableDefinition`; the simplified code preserves current behavior and test coverage for routing, pipeline ordering, and fingerprint reads.
steps-to-verify: Delete or refactor redundant `EffectiveSchema` constants and repeated middleware guard code, keeping one shared definition and one authoritative validation point.
Update or add tests so pipeline ordering and fingerprint reader behavior still pass after the cleanup.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PipelineOrderingTests|FullyQualifiedName~ResolveDmsInstanceMiddlewareTests|FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests"`

description: Move `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` so it still runs immediately after instance routing but only after non-schema-dependent request-shape validation that must preserve current error precedence. Acceptance: malformed or unknown routes continue to return the existing `404`/`400` responses from path parsing and route validation instead of attempting a database fingerprint read; valid routed requests still validate `dms.EffectiveSchema` before any schema-dependent middleware or handlers execute; pipeline-ordering tests explicitly lock in the new ordering.
steps-to-verify: Adjust the common pipeline ordering so fingerprint validation occurs after request parsing/route validation steps that do not require schema data, while remaining before schema-dependent middleware and handlers.
Add or update tests proving invalid paths and invalid route shapes do not invoke `IDatabaseFingerprintReader` and still return the same status codes as before.

Update pipeline ordering tests to assert the exact relative position of `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` versus `ResolveDmsInstanceMiddleware`, `ParsePathMiddleware`, and the first schema-dependent step.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PipelineOrderingTests|FullyQualifiedName~ParsePathMiddlewareTests|FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware|FullyQualifiedName~ResolveDmsInstanceMiddlewareTests"`

description: Make the malformed-fingerprint operator guidance consistent with the actual cache behavior for permanent validation failures. Acceptance: if malformed `dms.EffectiveSchema` content is cached as a permanent per-connection-string failure, the `503` response and logs explicitly state that DMS must be restarted after the database is repaired; user-facing remediation text and test assertions stay aligned with the implemented behavior.
steps-to-verify: Choose a single supported recovery model for malformed fingerprint failures: either keep permanent caching and update the problem-details/logging text to require restart after repair, or change the provider to allow recovery without restart.
Add or update middleware/provider tests that assert the exact remediation text and recovery behavior for malformed fingerprint failures.
Verify that provisioning-missing guidance and malformed-fingerprint guidance are distinct and both remain actionable.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DatabaseFingerprintProviderTests|FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddlewareValidationErrorTests|FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddlewareMissingTableTests|FullyQualifiedName~ProblemDetailsResponseTests"`

description: Map malformed `dms.EffectiveSchema` schema-shape failures to the intended actionable `503` instead of allowing them to escape as generic `500`s. Acceptance: when the table exists but the runtime fingerprint read cannot execute because required fingerprint columns are missing, renamed, or otherwise no longer match the expected projection, the backend converts that failure into `DatabaseFingerprintValidationException` (or an equivalent fingerprint-contract exception) and `ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware` returns the malformed-fingerprint problem-details response; missing table and missing singleton-row cases continue to return the existing database-not-provisioned response; transient connectivity/command failures that are not fingerprint-contract violations still follow the non-validation failure path.
steps-to-verify: Update the fingerprint reader path so projection/reader setup failures caused by malformed `dms.EffectiveSchema` structure are classified as permanent fingerprint-validation failures before they reach generic exception handling.

Add backend tests covering schema-shape failures that occur before `ReadValidatedFingerprintAsync()`, such as a required column missing from the executed `SELECT` result or a provider error caused by the malformed projection.

Add middleware or pipeline tests proving those failures produce the same `503` malformed-fingerprint response contract as duplicate rows and invalid fingerprint values, and do not fall through `CoreExceptionLoggingMiddleware` as an unknown error.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DatabaseFingerprintReaderSupportTests"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddlewareValidationErrorTests|FullyQualifiedName~ProblemDetailsResponseTests"`

description: Fail fast when relational-backend fingerprint validation is enabled but no dialect-specific `IDatabaseFingerprintReader` is registered, instead of misreporting the host misconfiguration as 'database not provisioned'. Acceptance: a host that sets `UseRelationalBackend=true` without registering a real fingerprint reader receives a configuration/service-registration failure that clearly identifies the missing backend reader; that failure is distinct from the operational 'database not provisioned' `503`; the normal ASP.NET Core composition path that registers PostgreSQL or MSSQL readers continues to work unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Replace the silent no-op fallback registration or behavior with an explicit fail-fast mechanism when the relational backend is enabled and the effective host composition does not provide a concrete fingerprint reader.

Add tests covering the misconfigured-host scenario and assert that the observed failure identifies missing service registration or backend configuration rather than returning the database-not-provisioned problem type.
Add or update a composition test for the normal frontend builder path to confirm PostgreSQL and MSSQL hosts still resolve a concrete `IDatabaseFingerprintReader` implementation.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ValidateDatabaseFingerprintMiddleware|FullyQualifiedName~PipelineOrderingTests"`

description: Align provisioning-time `dms.EffectiveSchema` invariants with the stricter runtime fingerprint invariants so provisioning cannot succeed on metadata that runtime will later reject. Acceptance: the provisioning path validates the same contract that runtime enforces for `ApiSchemaFormatVersion`, `EffectiveSchemaHash`, `ResourceKeyCount`, and `ResourceKeySeedHash`; malformed values such as empty `ApiSchemaFormatVersion`, non-lowercase or wrong-length `EffectiveSchemaHash`, negative `ResourceKeyCount`, or wrong-length seed hashes fail during DDL emission, preflight validation, or provisioning rather than surfacing only on first request; runtime and provisioning validation logic are centralized or otherwise kept in lockstep by tests.
steps-to-verify: Extend DDL/provisioning validation so `dms.EffectiveSchema` writes and re-validation check all runtime-required invariants, not just singleton/hash uniqueness and resource-key count/hash matching.
Add unit tests for DDL/provisioning validation failures covering empty `ApiSchemaFormatVersion`, malformed `EffectiveSchemaHash`, and other runtime-rejected metadata states.
Add or update tests that prove the runtime validator and provisioning validator reject the same malformed fingerprint inputs with deterministic diagnostics.
